 {"id":"bd-6e9.9","title":"Bubbles Widget Test Suite","description":"Comprehensive test suite for all bubbles widgets. This ensures every widget is thoroughly tested with unit tests, property tests, integration tests, and e2e scenarios.

## Testing Philosophy
Every widget must be tested at multiple levels:
1. **Unit tests** - Individual method correctness
2. **Property tests** - Invariant verification with random inputs
3. **Integration tests** - Widget composition and interaction
4. **E2E tests** - Full user workflow simulation
5. **Snapshot tests** - Visual regression detection

## Test Requirements by Widget

### TextArea (bd-6e9.1)
Unit tests:
- `test_insert_single_char` - Insert at various cursor positions
- `test_insert_multiline` - Insert text containing newlines
- `test_delete_backward` - Backspace at line start joins lines
- `test_delete_forward` - Delete at line end joins lines
- `test_cursor_movement_horizontal` - Left/right wraps at line boundaries
- `test_cursor_movement_vertical` - Up/down maintains visual column
- `test_selection_shift_arrow` - Selection with shift+arrows
- `test_selection_word` - Double-click word selection
- `test_selection_line` - Triple-click line selection
- `test_undo_single_char` - Undo groups by word boundaries
- `test_undo_paste` - Paste is single undo unit
- `test_redo_after_edit` - Redo branch is discarded on new edit
- `test_word_wrap_soft` - Soft wrap doesn't modify buffer
- `test_word_wrap_hard` - Hard wrap inserts actual newlines
- `test_viewport_scroll_cursor_follow` - Viewport follows cursor
- `test_unicode_grapheme_cluster` - ZWJ sequences treated as single char
- `test_char_limit_enforcement` - Can't exceed limit
- `test_validation_callback` - Validation prevents invalid input

Property tests:
- `prop_insert_delete_inverse` - Insert then delete restores state
- `prop_undo_redo_inverse` - Undo then redo restores state
- `prop_cursor_always_valid` - Cursor never outside buffer bounds
- `prop_selection_ordered` - Selection start <= end

Integration tests:
- `test_textarea_in_form` - TextArea with other form widgets
- `test_textarea_focus_blur` - Focus/blur event handling
- `test_textarea_bracketed_paste` - Large paste via bracketed paste

E2E tests:
- `e2e_edit_document` - Open, edit, save workflow
- `e2e_search_replace` - Find and replace across document

### Help Widget (bd-6e9.3)
Unit tests:
- `test_single_binding_render` - Key + description formatting
- `test_multiple_bindings_horizontal` - Separator between bindings
- `test_binding_truncation` - Ellipsis when exceeds width
- `test_grouped_bindings` - Group headers and sections
- `test_full_help_toggle` - Short vs full help modes

### Paginator (bd-6e9.4)
Unit tests:
- `test_dot_pagination_render` - Correct dots for each page
- `test_numeric_pagination` - 'N/M' format
- `test_range_pagination_ellipsis` - '1 2 3 ... 10' format
- `test_page_navigation_bounds` - Can't go below 0 or above max
- `test_items_on_page_calculation` - Correct item ranges

Property tests:
- `prop_page_always_valid` - Page number always in valid range

### Cursor Utilities (bd-6e9.7)
Unit tests:
- `test_visual_column_tabs` - Tab width affects visual column
- `test_visual_column_wide_chars` - CJK chars are 2 columns
- `test_move_word_left` - Stops at word boundaries
- `test_move_word_right` - Skips punctuation correctly
- `test_byte_to_char_offset` - Correct conversion for UTF-8

Property tests:
- `prop_byte_char_roundtrip` - byte->char->byte is identity
- `prop_visual_always_positive` - Visual column >= 0

### runeutil (bd-6e9.8)
Unit tests:
- `test_grapheme_count_ascii` - ASCII string length
- `test_grapheme_count_emoji` - ZWJ family is 1 grapheme
- `test_grapheme_slice` - Correct slicing at grapheme boundaries
- `test_word_boundary_english` - Word splits on spaces/punctuation
- `test_word_boundary_cjk` - CJK characters are word boundaries
- `test_display_width_ascii` - ASCII is 1 cell per char
- `test_display_width_cjk` - CJK is 2 cells per char
- `test_truncate_to_width` - Truncates at grapheme boundaries

Property tests:
- `prop_truncate_never_exceeds` - Result width <= max_width
- `prop_grapheme_count_non_negative` - Count >= 0

## Logging Requirements
All tests must use structured logging with:
- `tracing::debug!` for test setup/teardown
- `tracing::info!` for key assertions
- `tracing::warn!` for unexpected but non-fatal conditions
- Test failures dump full state snapshot to logs

## Coverage Targets
- Line coverage: >= 90%
- Branch coverage: >= 85%
- Mutation testing survival: <= 20%

## Test Harness
Use `ftui-test` harness that provides:
- Deterministic time (frozen or controlled advancement)
- Mock terminal capabilities
- Input simulation API
- Output capture and assertion

Dependencies: All bubbles widgets, Testing infrastructure (bd-10i.11)


## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Snapshot tests cover each bubbles widget’s core render behavior.
- [ ] Interaction/unit tests cover state machines (timer/stopwatch/text editing).
- [ ] Tests are deterministic and failure output is actionable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:15:20.100943766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T00:31:45.272685410Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6e9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:18:01.603690945Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6e9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-6e9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:18:04.060257239Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6e9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-6e9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:18:04.692052140Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6e9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-6e9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:18:05.281609862Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6e9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-6e9.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:18:05.881517041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6e9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-6e9.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:18:06.483627147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"bd-6e9.9","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6qxh","title":"Frame Caching with Smart Invalidation","description":"# Frame Caching with Smart Invalidation

## Background & Motivation

Many widgets are static most of the time:
- Headers and footers
- Borders and decorations
- Rarely-changing status bars

Re-rendering them every frame wastes CPU.

**The solution:**
- Cache rendered widget output
- Invalidate on model change or resize
- Composite cached content with live content

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Requires bd-10i.3.6 (Buffer with scissor/opacity stacks)
- Complements bd-1iqn (Render Budget)

## Technical Design

```rust
pub struct CachedWidget<W: Widget> {
    inner: W,
    cache: Option<CachedBuffer>,
    last_area: Option<Rect>,
    dirty: bool,
}

pub struct CachedBuffer {
    buffer: Buffer,
    hash: u64,  // Content hash for change detection
}

impl<W: Widget> CachedWidget<W> {
    pub fn new(widget: W) -> Self { ... }
    pub fn mark_dirty(&mut self) { self.dirty = true; }
    
    pub fn render(&mut self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        let needs_render = self.dirty 
            || self.last_area != Some(area)
            || self.cache.is_none();
            
        if needs_render {
            let mut cache_buf = Buffer::empty(area);
            self.inner.render(area, &mut cache_buf);
            self.cache = Some(CachedBuffer::new(cache_buf));
            self.last_area = Some(area);
            self.dirty = false;
        }
        
        if let Some(cache) = &self.cache {
            buf.merge(&cache.buffer);
        }
    }
}
```

## Logging Requirements

```rust
// Cache hit (TRACE level)
tracing::trace!(widget = name, "Cache hit, using cached buffer");

// Cache miss (DEBUG level)
tracing::debug!(widget = name, reason = ?reason, "Cache miss, re-rendering");

// Cache invalidated (DEBUG level)
tracing::debug!(widget = name, "Cache invalidated via mark_dirty()");
```

## E2E Tests

```bash
# Test cache hit rate
./ftui-harness --test-mode cache-stats --duration 10s --static-widgets 5
# Assert: cache hit rate > 95%

# Test memory bounds
./ftui-harness --test-mode cache-memory --cached-widgets 100
# Assert: memory < 10MB for 100 widgets

# Verify correctness
./ftui-harness --test-mode cache-verify --compare-to-uncached
# Assert: output identical
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CachedWidget<W> wrapper implemented
- [ ] Caching works for static widgets
- [ ] Invalidation on mark_dirty()
- [ ] Resize triggers re-render
- [ ] Memory usage bounded
- [ ] Content hash detects changes
- [ ] Integration with widget tree
- [ ] No stale content ever displayed
- [ ] All tests pass","notes":"## Cache Safety Guidelines

### When to Use CachedWidget
- Static content (headers, footers, borders)
- Content that changes infrequently
- Expensive rendering operations

### When NOT to Use CachedWidget
- Dynamic content (logs, timers, animations)
- Content that depends on external state
- Small/fast widgets (caching overhead > render cost)

### Cache Coherence
The cache can become stale if:
1. Widget props change without mark_dirty()
2. External state affects rendering
3. Theme/style changes globally

Solution: Use automatic dirty detection via content hash:
```rust
impl<W: Widget + Hash> CachedWidget<W> {
    fn render(&mut self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        let current_hash = self.inner.content_hash();
        
        let needs_render = self.dirty
            || self.last_area != Some(area)
            || self.cache.as_ref().map(|c| c.hash) != Some(current_hash);
        
        // ...
    }
}
```

### Memory Management
```rust
impl<W: Widget> CachedWidget<W> {
    /// Drop cache to free memory
    pub fn clear_cache(&mut self) {
        self.cache = None;
    }
    
    /// Check cache memory usage
    pub fn cache_size(&self) -> usize {
        self.cache.as_ref().map(|c| c.buffer.memory_size()).unwrap_or(0)
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-7u8","title":"Implement cursor save/restore strategy (DEC vs ANSI vs emulated)","description":"# Implement Cursor Save/Restore Strategy

## Background
Plan Section 9.3 describes a critical cursor save/restore strategy for inline mode robustness. Terminals differ in which save/restore sequences they support reliably.

## Problem Statement
Inline mode requires saving cursor position before drawing UI and restoring after. However:
- DEC save/restore (\`ESC 7\` / \`ESC 8\`) is preferred but not universal
- ANSI save/restore (\`CSI s\` / \`CSI u\`) is an alternative
- Some terminals/muxes have quirks with either
- Must handle failures gracefully

## Layered Strategy (from Plan)

### Layer 1: DEC Save/Restore (Preferred)
\`\`\`rust
const DEC_SAVE: &[u8] = b"\x1b7";    // ESC 7 (DECSC)
const DEC_RESTORE: &[u8] = b"\x1b8"; // ESC 8 (DECRC)
\`\`\`
- Most widely supported on modern terminals
- Saves more state (cursor position, attributes, charset)
- Works in tmux/screen with passthrough

### Layer 2: ANSI Save/Restore (Fallback)
\`\`\`rust
const ANSI_SAVE: &[u8] = b"\x1b[s";    // CSI s
const ANSI_RESTORE: &[u8] = b"\x1b[u"; // CSI u
\`\`\`
- Less widely supported
- Only saves cursor position (not attributes)
- May conflict with some terminal modes

### Layer 3: Emulated (Last Resort)
\`\`\`rust
// Track cursor position explicitly
struct CursorTracker {
    x: u16,
    y: u16,
}

impl CursorTracker {
    fn save(&mut self, current_x: u16, current_y: u16) {
        self.x = current_x;
        self.y = current_y;
    }
    
    fn restore(&self, writer: &mut impl Write) -> io::Result<()> {
        write!(writer, "\x1b[{};{}H", self.y + 1, self.x + 1)
    }
}
\`\`\`
- Works everywhere that supports CUP
- Requires tracking cursor position throughout
- More overhead but guaranteed to work

## Strategy Selection
\`\`\`rust
pub enum CursorSaveStrategy {
    Dec,       // ESC 7 / ESC 8
    Ansi,      // CSI s / CSI u
    Emulated,  // Track and CUP
    Auto,      // Detect best for environment
}

impl CursorSaveStrategy {
    pub fn detect(caps: &TerminalCapabilities) -> Self {
        // Most terminals support DEC
        if !caps.in_screen {
            return Self::Dec;
        }
        // Screen has quirks, try ANSI
        Self::Ansi
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Implementation
\`\`\`rust
pub struct CursorManager {
    strategy: CursorSaveStrategy,
    saved_position: Option<(u16, u16)>,  // For emulated mode
}

impl CursorManager {
    pub fn save(&mut self, writer: &mut impl Write, current_pos: (u16, u16)) -> io::Result<()> {
        match self.strategy {
            CursorSaveStrategy::Dec => writer.write_all(DEC_SAVE),
            CursorSaveStrategy::Ansi => writer.write_all(ANSI_SAVE),
            CursorSaveStrategy::Emulated => {
                self.saved_position = Some(current_pos);
                Ok(())
            }
            CursorSaveStrategy::Auto => {
                // Try DEC, it's most common
                writer.write_all(DEC_SAVE)
            }
        }
    }
    
    pub fn restore(&mut self, writer: &mut impl Write) -> io::Result<()> {
        match self.strategy {
            CursorSaveStrategy::Dec => writer.write_all(DEC_RESTORE),
            CursorSaveStrategy::Ansi => writer.write_all(ANSI_RESTORE),
            CursorSaveStrategy::Emulated => {
                if let Some((x, y)) = self.saved_position {
                    write!(writer, "\x1b[{};{}H", y + 1, x + 1)?;
                }
                Ok(())
            }
            CursorSaveStrategy::Auto => writer.write_all(DEC_RESTORE),
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

## PTY Test: Cursor Restoration
Test that verifies cursor is correctly restored:
1. Move cursor to known position
2. Save cursor
3. Move cursor elsewhere
4. Restore cursor
5. Query position (DSR) and verify matches saved position

## Tests Required
- [ ] DEC save/restore works on xterm-like terminals
- [ ] ANSI save/restore works as fallback
- [ ] Emulated mode tracks and restores correctly
- [ ] Auto-detection chooses appropriate strategy
- [ ] PTY test verifies cursor contract per strategy

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CursorManager implemented with all three strategies
- [ ] Auto-detection based on TerminalCapabilities
- [ ] Integrated into InlinePresenter
- [ ] PTY tests pass for all strategies
- [ ] Documented which terminals use which strategy

## Location
\`ftui-core/src/cursor.rs\`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:18:52.006565139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T07:17:13.750166770Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T07:17:13.750141643Z","close_reason":"Implemented cursor save/restore strategy with DEC (ESC 7/8), ANSI (CSI s/u), and Emulated modes. CursorManager handles save/restore with auto-detection based on TerminalCapabilities. 15 unit tests cover all modes and edge cases.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7u8","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:18:59.327962044Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-9xbn","title":"Layout Constraint Debugger","description":"# Layout Constraint Debugger

## Background & Motivation

Layout bugs are notoriously hard to debug:
- Constraints are implicit in code
- Computed values aren't visible
- Overflows and underflows silent

**The solution:**
- Visual debugger for layout constraints
- Shows requested vs received sizes
- Highlights overflow/underflow in red
- Tree view of layout hierarchy

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Requires bd-3ky.4 (highlight theme system)
- Requires bd-yzu (Flex layout with constraint solving)
- Complements bd-17nn (Widget Introspection Debug Overlay)

## Technical Design

```rust
pub struct LayoutDebugger {
    enabled: bool,
    records: Vec<LayoutRecord>,
}

pub struct LayoutRecord {
    widget_name: String,
    area_requested: Rect,
    area_received: Rect,
    constraints: LayoutConstraints,
    children: Vec<LayoutRecord>,
}

pub struct LayoutConstraints {
    min_width: u16,
    max_width: u16,
    min_height: u16,
    max_height: u16,
}

impl LayoutDebugger {
    pub fn record(&mut self, name: &str, requested: Rect, received: Rect, constraints: LayoutConstraints) { ... }
    pub fn render_debug(&self, buf: &mut Buffer) { ... }
    pub fn export_dot(&self) -> String { ... }  // Graphviz export
}
```

## Logging Requirements

```rust
// Constraint violation (WARN level)
tracing::warn!(
    widget = name,
    requested = ?requested,
    received = ?received,
    "Layout constraint violation"
);

// Layout computed (DEBUG level)
tracing::debug!(
    widget = name,
    constraints = ?constraints,
    result = ?area,
    "Layout computed"
);
```

## E2E Tests

```bash
# Enable layout debugger and run complex layout
./ftui-harness --layout-debugger --layout complex_grid

# Export constraint graph
./ftui-harness --layout-debugger --export-dot layout.dot

# Verify no constraint violations
./scripts/verify-layout.sh --assert-no-violations

# Visual regression test
./ftui-harness --layout-debugger --screenshot layout_debug.png
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Constraint recording works for all widgets
- [ ] Overflow highlighted in red
- [ ] Underflow highlighted in yellow
- [ ] Computed values visible alongside constraints
- [ ] Tree view of layout hierarchy
- [ ] Graphviz export for documentation
- [ ] Zero overhead when disabled
- [ ] All tests pass","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:12:16.766323253Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:22:57.282357812Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9xbn","depends_on_id":"bd-3ky.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:40:41.933853646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9xbn","depends_on_id":"bd-yzu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:13:18.710206757Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-agng","title":"Implement recovery mechanism for budget upgrade","description":"# Implement recovery mechanism for budget upgrade

## Background
Part of Render Budget Enforcement (bd-1iqn). When load decreases, the system should recover to higher fidelity. This must be gradual and stable.

## Technical Design

```rust
/// Budget recovery tracker
pub struct BudgetRecovery {
    /// Current degradation level
    current_level: DegradationLevel,
    /// Frames at current level under budget
    stable_frames: u32,
    /// Required stable frames before upgrade
    threshold: u32,
    /// Frames since last degradation
    frames_since_degrade: u32,
    /// Minimum frames before allowing upgrade after degradation
    min_recovery_delay: u32,
}

impl BudgetRecovery {
    pub fn new(threshold: u32) -> Self {
        Self {
            current_level: DegradationLevel::Full,
            stable_frames: 0,
            threshold,
            frames_since_degrade: u32::MAX / 2,  // Start as if long stable
            min_recovery_delay: 10,
        }
    }
    
    /// Record a frame that finished under budget
    pub fn record_under_budget(&mut self) {
        self.stable_frames = self.stable_frames.saturating_add(1);
        self.frames_since_degrade = self.frames_since_degrade.saturating_add(1);
    }
    
    /// Record a frame that exceeded budget
    pub fn record_over_budget(&mut self) {
        self.stable_frames = 0;
    }
    
    /// Check if we should upgrade to higher fidelity
    pub fn should_upgrade(&self) -> bool {
        self.current_level != DegradationLevel::Full
            && self.stable_frames >= self.threshold
            && self.frames_since_degrade >= self.min_recovery_delay
    }
    
    /// Perform upgrade
    pub fn upgrade(&mut self) {
        if self.current_level != DegradationLevel::Full {
            let old = self.current_level;
            self.current_level = self.current_level.upgrade();
            self.stable_frames = 0;  // Reset stability counter
            
            tracing::info!(
                from = ?old,
                to = ?self.current_level,
                "Upgrading render fidelity after stable performance"
            );
        }
    }
    
    /// Record degradation (resets recovery progress)
    pub fn on_degrade(&mut self, new_level: DegradationLevel) {
        self.current_level = new_level;
        self.stable_frames = 0;
        self.frames_since_degrade = 0;
    }
}
```

## Why Recovery Delay?
- Prevents ping-pong between levels
- Allows system to stabilize after load spike
- Users see gradual improvement, not flickering

## Test Requirements
- [ ] stable_frames increments on under_budget
- [ ] stable_frames resets on over_budget
- [ ] should_upgrade requires threshold AND delay
- [ ] upgrade() advances level and logs
- [ ] on_degrade() resets all counters

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] BudgetRecovery struct implemented

 {"id":"bd-b8m5","title":"Define CapturedError struct for panic information","description":"# Define CapturedError struct for panic information

## Background
Part of Widget Error Boundaries (bd-1euu). When a widget panics, we need to capture comprehensive diagnostic information for debugging.

## Technical Design

```rust
use std::any::Any;
use std::backtrace::Backtrace;
use std::time::Instant;

/// Captured error from a widget panic
#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
pub struct CapturedError {
    /// Error message extracted from panic
    pub message: String,
    
    /// Name of the widget that panicked
    pub widget_name: &'static str,
    
    /// Backtrace at panic point (if available)
    pub backtrace: Option<String>,
    
    /// When the panic occurred
    pub timestamp: Instant,
    
    /// Area the widget was rendering when it panicked
    pub area: Rect,
    
    /// Render phase when panic occurred
    pub phase: RenderPhase,
    
    /// Parent widget chain (for context)
    pub widget_path: Vec<&'static str>,
}

/// Phase of rendering when error occurred
#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum RenderPhase {
    /// During layout/constraint solving
    Layout,
    /// During actual buffer writes
    Render,
    /// During event handling
    Update,
}

impl CapturedError {
    /// Create from a caught panic
    pub fn from_panic(
        panic_info: Box<dyn Any + Send>,
        widget_name: &'static str,
        area: Rect,
        phase: RenderPhase,
    ) -> Self {
        // Extract message from panic payload
        let message = if let Some(s) = panic_info.downcast_ref::<&str>() {
            s.to_string()
        } else if let Some(s) = panic_info.downcast_ref::<String>() {
            s.clone()
        } else {
            "Unknown panic".to_string()
        };
        
        // Capture backtrace
        let backtrace = match std::backtrace::Backtrace::capture().status() {
            std::backtrace::BacktraceStatus::Captured => {
                Some(std::backtrace::Backtrace::capture().to_string())
            }
            _ => None,
        };
        
        Self {
            message,
            widget_name,
            backtrace,
            timestamp: Instant::now(),
            area,
            phase,
            widget_path: Vec::new(),
        }
    }
    
    /// Add parent widget to path (for nested boundaries)
    pub fn with_parent(mut self, parent: &'static str) -> Self {
        self.widget_path.push(parent);
        self
    }
    
    /// Format for logging
    pub fn log_format(&self) -> String {
        format!(
            "Widget panic in {}: {} at {:?}",
            self.widget_name,
            self.message,
            self.area
        )
    }
    
    /// Format for user display (truncated)
    pub fn display_format(&self, max_len: usize) -> String {
        let msg: String = self.message.chars().take(max_len.saturating_sub(4)).collect();
        format!("[!] {}...", msg)
    }
}
```

## Why This Design
1. **Rich context**: Widget name, area, phase all captured
2. **Backtrace optional**: May not be available in all builds
3. **Widget path**: Shows nesting for complex UIs
4. **Two formats**: Full for logs, truncated for display

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CapturedError struct with all fields
- [ ] from_panic extracts message from Any
- [ ] Backtrace captured when available
- [ ] log_format() for tracing
- [ ] display_format() for UI","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:18:16.386273542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T19:28:09.284105824Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T19:28:09.284072601Z","close_reason":"CapturedError struct already implemented as part of bd-1euu in ftui-widgets/src/error_boundary.rs with message, widget_name, area, timestamp fields and from_panic constructor","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b8m5","depends_on_id":"bd-1euu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:18:40.505924462Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-bo6w","title":"Define ProbeableCapability enum and probe sequences","description":"# Define ProbeableCapability enum and probe sequences

## Background
Part of Terminal Capability Auto-Upgrade (bd-3227). Define capabilities that can be probed at runtime.

## Technical Design

```rust
/// Capabilities that can be probed at runtime
#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Debug)]
pub enum ProbeableCapability {
    /// TrueColor (24-bit RGB) support
    TrueColor,
    /// Synchronized output (mode 2026)
    SynchronizedOutput,
    /// OSC 8 hyperlinks
    Hyperlinks,
    /// Kitty keyboard protocol
    KittyKeyboard,
    /// Sixel graphics
    Sixel,
    /// iTerm2 inline images
    ItermImages,
}

impl ProbeableCapability {
    /// All probeable capabilities
    pub fn all() -> &'static [Self] {
        &[
            Self::TrueColor,
            Self::SynchronizedOutput,
            Self::Hyperlinks,
            Self::KittyKeyboard,
            Self::Sixel,
            Self::ItermImages,
        ]
    }
    
    /// Get probe sequence bytes
    pub fn probe_sequence(&self) -> &'static [u8] {
        match self {
            // DA2 - Device Attributes Level 2
            Self::TrueColor => b"\x1b[>c",
            // Query mode 2026
            Self::SynchronizedOutput => b"\x1b[?2026$p",
            // Query XTGETTCAP for hyperlinks
            Self::Hyperlinks => b"\x1bP+q485950\x1b\\",
            // Query keyboard mode
            Self::KittyKeyboard => b"\x1b[?u",
            // Query Sixel support via DA1
            Self::Sixel => b"\x1b[c",
            // iTerm2 specific query
            Self::ItermImages => b"\x1b]1337;ReportCellSize\x07",
        }
    }
    
    /// Default timeout for this probe
    pub fn timeout(&self) -> Duration {
        match self {
            // Quick probes
            Self::TrueColor | Self::SynchronizedOutput => Duration::from_millis(100),
            // May need more time
            Self::Hyperlinks | Self::KittyKeyboard => Duration::from_millis(200),
            // Slower responses typical
            Self::Sixel | Self::ItermImages => Duration::from_millis(300),
        }
    }
    
    /// Convert to general Capability for upgrade
    pub fn to_capability(&self) -> Capability {
        match self {
            Self::TrueColor => Capability::TrueColor,
            Self::SynchronizedOutput => Capability::SynchronizedOutput,
            Self::Hyperlinks => Capability::Hyperlinks,
            Self::KittyKeyboard => Capability::KittyKeyboard,
            Self::Sixel => Capability::Sixel,
            Self::ItermImages => Capability::Images,
        }
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 6 probe capabilities defined
- [ ] Probe sequences are correct per terminal specs
- [ ] Timeouts are conservative but practical
- [ ] Mapping to general Capability works","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:23:32.604035018Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:43:39.362989807Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:43:39.362925626Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bo6w","depends_on_id":"bd-3227","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:24:23.887473017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
